web www.e-health-suisse.ch The CH EMR implementation guide describes the FHIR representation of the electronic emergency record in Switzerland. It is dependent on CH Core and CH Term , which describe the Swiss-specific context, particularly in relation to the Swiss Electronic Patient Record (EPR).
This implementation guide is based on the concept for the exchange format of the electronic emergency record ( de , fr ).
web www.e-health-suisse.ch The CH EMR implementation guide describes the FHIR representation of the electronic emergency record in Switzerland. It is dependent on CH Core and CH Term , which describe the Swiss-specific context, particularly in relation to the Swiss Electronic Patient Record (EPR).
This implementation guide is based on the concept for the exchange format of the electronic emergency record ( de , fr ).
web fhir.ch The CH EMR is primarily derived from the CH IPS (International Patient Summary) , which provides the foundational clinical profiles for patient summaries in Switzerland. CH IPS itself is based on the international IPS UV specification by HL7, ensuring cross-border interoperability.
web fhir.ch Both CH EMR and CH IPS depend on CH Core , which defines the Swiss-specific base profiles (e.g. patient, practitioner, organization) including Swiss identifiers such as AHV, GLN and EPR-SPID. CH Term provides the shared Swiss terminologies, value sets and code systems used across all Swiss FHIR implementation guides.
web fhir.ch Both CH EMR and CH IPS depend on CH Core , which defines the Swiss-specific base profiles (e.g. patient, practitioner, organization) including Swiss identifiers such as AHV, GLN and EPR-SPID. CH Term provides the shared Swiss terminologies, value sets and code systems used across all Swiss FHIR implementation guides.
web fhir.ch For the medication content, CH EMR additionally depends on CH EMED , the Swiss eMedikation specification. CH EMR's Medication Statement reuses the CH EMED dosage profiles ( ch-emed-dosage and ch-emed-dosage-split ) so that dosing schedules in the Emergency Record follow the same structure as the rest of the Swiss eMedikation ecosystem.
web www.hl7.ch This implementation guide is under STU ballot by HL7 Switzerland from August 4th, 2026 until end of 2026.
Please add your feedback via the ‘Propose a change’-link in the footer on the page where you have comments.
web creativecommons.org This document is licensed under Creative Commons "No Rights Reserved" ( CC0 ).
web www.iso.org ISO maintains the copyright on the country codes, and controls its use carefully. For further details see the ISO 3166 web page: https://www.iso.org/iso-3166-country-codes.html
Show Usage
  • ISO 3166-1 Codes for the representation of names of countries and their subdivisions — Part 1: Country code : CH_EMR , ChEmrAdvanceDirectiveTypeVS ... Show 24 more , ChEmrBodyStructure, ChEmrBodyStructureReference, ChEmrClinicalDocumentTypesVS, ChEmrCognitiveDisabilityTypesVS, ChEmrComposition, ChEmrDevice, ChEmrDeviceUseStatement, ChEmrDocument, ChEmrDocumentReference, ChEmrDocumentReferenceAdvanceDirective, ChEmrFlagRiskToHealthcarePersonnel, ChEmrImplantTypeVS, ChEmrMedicationStatement, ChEmrMriSafetyStatusCS, ChEmrMriSafetyStatusVS, ChEmrObservationCognitiveDisability, ChEmrObservationPhysicalDisability, ChEmrObservationResuscitationStatus, ChEmrPhysicalDisabilityTypesVS, ChEmrRelatedPerson, ChEmrResuscitationStatusVS, ChEmrRiskToHealthcarePersonnelVS, EuropeanCommissionUDI and GS1DeviceIdentifier

web www.fedlex.admin.ch Swiss Electronic Patient Record (EPR) — EPDG . When a CH EMR document is published into or retrieved from a Swiss reference community, the Federal Act on the Electronic Patient Record (EPDG / LDEP) and its implementing ordinances (EPDV, EPDV-EDI) apply in full — patient consent, access-control policies, audit logging, identification levels and accredited reference-community gateways.
web www.fedlex.admin.ch Outside the EPR . Even outside the EPR setting (e.g. point-to-point exchange between care providers, mobile EMR carriers, internal records), the Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP / nDSG) applies. Health data is "sensitive personal data" under FADP article 5 and triggers the stricter lawful-basis, transparency and data-subject-rights requirements that follow from that classification.
web www.fedlex.admin.ch Federal Act on the Electronic Patient Record (EPDG / LDEP)
web www.fedlex.admin.ch Federal Act on Data Protection (FADP / nDSG, 2023)
web www.e-health-suisse.ch eHealth Suisse — recommendations and policy documents
